title: Where Billionaires Summer, a Gardener Died in the Snow
description: A landscaper’s difficult life and lonely death reveal the human cost behind the Hamptons’ manicured landscape.
author: Rukmini Callimachi and Victor J. Blue

title: Mamdani Burns Allies in Making a Big Bet for Congress and the Left
description: The strength of the mayor’s political brand will be tested on Tuesday, when his slate of leftist congressional candidates takes aim at Democratic incumbents.
author: Nicholas Fandos and Sally Goldenberg

title: Father and Uncle of Slain New York Girl Given Life Sentences in Pakistan
description: The killing of the victim, a 14-year-old girl from Yonkers who was known for posting videos on TikTok, was premeditated, a court ruled.
author: Zia ur-Rehman

title: You Can’t Be a Superpower Without Allies
description: Even the most powerful state in the world is not all that powerful when it decides to go it alone.
author: Oona A. Hathaway

title: A Diocese Tries to Protect Its 29-Foot Jesus From Trump’s Border Wall
description: In a battle of symbols, the Catholics of Las Cruces, N.M., argue that religious freedom should stop the wall from scarring a mountain that has attracted pilgrims for nearly a century.
author: Reis Thebault and Paul Ratje

title: Trump Administration Shuttered a Criminal Probe Into Fraudster’s Clemency
description: Federal prosecutors had been examining the circumstances behind the commutation of David Gentile’s sentence. He was aided by a Catholic priest friendly with the president.
author: Kenneth P. Vogel, Nicole Hong and William K. Rashbaum

title: Elon Musk Is Too Rich. We’ve Known That Since Plato.
description: The philosopher made a radical proposal about wealth.
author: David Lay Williams

title: The Dad Movie Canon: Films That Define Dad Cinema
description: What is it about a certain kind of film that appeals to fathers? Our writer took a stab at defining Dad Cinema and rounding up its greatest hits.
author: Jason Bailey

title: Trump Says Reflecting Pool Will Likely Need to Be Drained
description: The pool has taken on clouds of algae after a hasty renovation. A three-time Olympian was charged with destroying government property after he says he touched one of the strands of blue paint peeling off the pool’s bottom.
author: Minho Kim

title: Mona Khalil, Defender of Sea Turtles, Killed in an Israeli Strike in Lebanon
description: For a quarter century, Ms. Khalil ran a guesthouse and worked to protect endangered sea turtles who every summer lay their eggs on a stretch of beach near Tyre, Lebanon.
author: Ishani Desai

title: Cottage Cheese Shortages Are Driven by TikTok and ‘Protein-Maxxing’
description: TikTok and “protein-maxxing” are driving a cottage cheese squeeze that’s left some consumers desperate.
author: Niko Gallogly and Ahmed Gaber

title: Creature From the Green Lagoon
description: Trump’s slimy, stinky swamp within the swamp.
author: Maureen Dowd

title: Under a Turtle Shell, a Stunning New Home for Shakespeare
description: After 38 years in a tent, Hudson Valley Shakespeare opens one of the most spectacular outdoor performance spaces in the country.
author: Jesse Green

title: ‘A Literal Coverup’: What Is the Kennedy Center Hiding Behind Those Tarps?
description: The arts center says it pulled President Trump’s name from the building front. But the result remains hidden by tarps, prompting questions and speculation.
author: Elizabeth Williamson

title: Disability Groups Fear RFK Jr.’s New Special Education Role
description: Alienated by Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s claims about autism, advocates for disabled students are sounding the alarm about the Trump administration's shifting special education programs to his department.
author: Michael C. Bender and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

title: James Burrows, Master of the TV Sitcom, Dies at 85
description: Beloved by actors, he helped create “Cheers” and directed more than 1,000 episodes of hit shows like “Taxi,” “Friends” and “The Big Bang Theory.”
author: Glenn Rifkin

title: Trump’s Reflecting Pool Is Plagued by Multiple Problems, From Green Algae to Broken Pipes
description: As National Park Service crews try to clean the algae that turned the water bright green, another problem has developed: The “American flag blue” coating is coming off.
author: Aishvarya Kavi

title: Trump Was Right. The War Ended With a Surrender.
description: Each generation seems destined to learn anew the lesson that when hawks declare that a war will be painless, beware.
author: Nicholas Kristof

title: We Need to Keep Funding Weird Science
description: Science turned a serendipitous finding about lizard venom into one of the most important drugs of the century, but that type of research is getting harder to do.
author: Jeff Coller

title: A Scandinavian Answer to John Singer Sargent Gets Her Due
description: Dismissed for decades by critics as a mere “fashion portraitist” of high-society ladies, Asta Norregaard is now the subject of a major new retrospective at Oslo’s National Museum.
author: Nina Siegal
